parentQuestion: A patient presents with severe, lancinating (stabbing) facial pain triggered by ...

scaffolds.q: The trigeminal nerve carries sensation from the face.
scaffolds.explain: CN V (trigeminal) provides sensory innervation to the face via three divisions: V1 (forehead), V2 (cheek), V3 (jaw). Let this settle:the coverage — from hairline to jaw, from nose to ear, it's the sentinel of facial sensation. When it malfunctions, the consequences are devastating.

scaffolds.q: Neuropathic pain can occur without tissue damage, arising from nerve dysfunction itself.
scaffolds.explain: Neuropathic pain originates from abnormal nerve signaling — the nerve itself becomes the source of pain, not an external injury. it's like a fire alarm that won't stop going off even though there's no fire. The warning system has become the threat.
